title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. pleiotrichum
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. pleiotrichum, also known as wood hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with yellow flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, wood hawkweed can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
